Our Programs

Three Programs.
One Mission.

Whether you're a clinician with an idea, an engineer solving a real problem, or a startup ready to scale — BMIIL has a structured program built for exactly where you are right now.

Programs

Three tracks.
All roads lead to impact.

Medtech Incubation

"From clinical insight to validated product."

Our flagship program. Built for healthcare, biotech, and medical device innovators who have identified a real clinical problem and are ready to build a solution with rigor — from prototyping through regulatory compliance to investor readiness.

Growth Stage · 6 Months

Built to scale.
Built to close.

The BMIIL Accelerator is for health and wellness startups that already have a product, early customers, or traction — and need structured, intensive support to reach the next order of magnitude.

6
Month Sprint
8
Seats Available
2×
Demo Days
3
Focused Sprints

Is This For You?

✓ This track is built for you if…

✓You have a health or wellness product that real customers have used
✓You understand your ICP and have validated the core value proposition
✓You're looking to raise your first institutional round — angel, pre-seed, or seed
✓You want to access hospital systems, pharma companies, or healthcare corporates as B2B partners
✓You need structured support with GTM, revenue operations, or channel partnerships

✗ Consider a different track if…

✗You're still building your first prototype — the Medtech Incubation track will serve you better
✗Your product is primarily an Ayurveda or traditional medicine innovation
✗You haven't yet identified a clear monetisation model or target customer segment
✗Your primary challenge is technical development, not growth or fundraising

Sprint Structure

Six months. Three focused sprints.

Sprint 1 · Month 1
Diagnosis & Positioning

An honest, structured audit of where you actually are. We stress-test your product, market understanding, team, and financials before building any growth plan.

→ Business model audit & P&L review
→ ICP validation & customer segmentation
→ Competitive positioning map
→ Fundraising readiness scorecard
→ 6-month OKR framework defined
Sprint 2 · Months 2–4
Growth & GTM Execution

Full execution sprint. Three months of structured growth activities — go-to-market, distribution, pilot partnerships, revenue operations, and investor pipeline.

→ GTM campaigns live
→ 3+ hospital or corporate pilots
→ Revenue ops system built
→ Investor pipeline of 20+
→ Mid-program demo day
Sprint 3 · Months 5–6
Fundraise & Scale

The final sprint is built around one outcome — closing capital. Investor deck refined, data room ready, term sheets negotiated.

→ Investor deck (final)
→ Data room complete
→ Demo Day pitch
→ Term sheet negotiations
→ Post-cohort investor introductions

Ready to apply for the Accelerator?

Applications are rolling. We spend more time with early applicants.

Apply for This Track →

Unique Differentiator · 12 Months

Ancient intelligence.
Modern evidence.

The only incubation track in North India dedicated to turning Ayurveda, herbal medicine, and traditional wellness knowledge into scientifically validated, commercially viable, globally competitive products — with rigorous evidence at the core.

$200B+
Global Herbal Market by 2030
₹75,000 Cr
India's Ayush Sector
<1%
Ayurvedic Products Clinically Validated
9,000+
Medicinal Plants Documented in India

The Opportunity

A global market.
Almost no credible pipeline.

The global market for herbal medicines, nutraceuticals, and traditional wellness products is approaching $200 billion. The demand has never been higher — in Europe, the US, Southeast Asia, and increasingly within India itself.

India has a head start no other country can replicate: 5,000 years of documented Ayurvedic knowledge, over 9,000 identified medicinal plants, and a Ministry of AYUSH with growing funding.

"Less than 1% of Ayurvedic formulations in the market today have been validated through modern clinical standards. That's not a reason to dismiss Ayurveda — it's the size of the opportunity."

$200B+
Global Herbal Market

Growing at 8–12% CAGR, driven by consumer demand for natural, evidence-backed wellness products

₹75,000 Cr
India's Ayush Sector

Target of ₹1.2 lakh crore by 2025 with government pushing hard on export promotion

60+
Countries with TM Policy

Regulatory pathways for traditional medicine products are opening globally

<1%
Products Validated

The gap between traditional knowledge and clinical evidence is the size of the opportunity

Is This For You?

✓ This track is built for you if…

✓You're an Ayurveda practitioner (BAMS/MD) or traditional medicine researcher
✓You want to validate traditional formulations or develop herbal nutraceuticals
✓You believe in the scientific rigor of evidence-based traditional medicine
✓You want to access AYUSH regulatory pathways and global markets
✓You have a formulation, compound, or traditional practice worth validating scientifically

✗ Consider a different track if…

✗You want to sell traditional products without clinical validation
✗Your primary innovation is a medical device or digital health tool — apply for Medtech
✗You already have a validated product with customers — consider the Accelerator
✗You're not open to subjecting traditional knowledge to modern scientific scrutiny

Ready to apply for the Ayush track?

8 seats. Rolling applications. Ancient knowledge deserves modern validation.

Apply for This Track →

Core Track · 12 Months

From clinical insight
to validated product.

Our flagship program for healthcare, biotech, and medical device innovators. If you've identified a real clinical problem and are serious about solving it — with rigour, regulatory awareness, and a plan to reach patients — this is your track.

12
Month Program
10
Seats Available
4
Structured Phases
500+
Bed Hospital Access

Is This For You?

✓ This track is built for you if…

✓You're a clinician, researcher, or engineer who has identified a specific, patient-validated clinical problem
✓You want to build a medical device, diagnostic tool, digital health solution, or biotech product
✓You're at idea, early research, or pre-prototype stage and need infrastructure to develop further
✓You want regulatory guidance (CDSCO, FDA) built into your development process from day one
✓You're willing to be physically present on the BIU campus for core program milestones

✗ Consider a different track if…

✗You already have a working product with early revenue — the Accelerator is a better fit
✗Your innovation is primarily in Ayurveda or traditional medicine — apply for the Ayush track
✗You're looking for a co-working space or a short-term mentorship programme
✗You haven't yet spent meaningful time with the clinical problem and the patients it affects

Curriculum

12 months. Four phases. Zero shortcuts.

The program is structured in four sequential phases, each with defined deliverables. You don't move to the next phase until the current one is complete and reviewed.

1
Months 1–2
Discovery & Problem Validation

Clinical immersion, problem statement refinement, initial market assessment. You'll spend the first two months in BIU's hospital wards and OPDs.

→ Validated Problem Statement
→ Stakeholder Interview Report
→ Competitive Landscape Map
→ Regulatory Pathway Scoping
2
Months 3–7
Build & Prototype

The longest phase. Full access to BIU's biotech, pharma, and medical device labs. You'll build, test, fail, and iterate — with technical advisors at every step.

→ Working Prototype / MVP
→ Lab Test Reports
→ IP Assessment
→ Technical Specification
3
Months 8–10
Validate & Regulate

Clinical validation with real patients through BIU's hospital. Regulatory filing preparation begins. IP protection strategy finalised.

→ Clinical Validation Report
→ CDSCO / FDA Filing Draft
→ Patent Filing (if applicable)
→ Regulatory Pathway Confirmed
4
Months 11–12
Demo Day & Launch

Investor presentations, partnership introductions, go-to-market execution. You leave with an investor deck, pilot customers, and introductions to our investor network.

→ Investor Deck
→ Demo Day Pitch
→ GTM Strategy
→ Investor Introductions

Ready to apply for the Medtech Incubation track?
